assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is denominated in a foreign currency and creates foreign exchange exposure.
The question concerns the accounting in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk that could affect profit or loss or, in limited cases, other comprehensive income.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk on recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 establish a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation, with effective hedge results recognised in other comprehensive income.

approaches.label: IAS 21 foreign exchange accounting without hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the baseline accounting for exchange differences on foreign-currency monetary items and for qualifying net investment items when hedge accounting is not applied.
